npcs.name: Morra Veld
npcs.location: Keth — Archive of Keth, Archive Quarter
npcs.physical: Small, white-haired, precise in movement with the economy of someone who has learned to conserve energy without losing presence. Her eyes are still sharp in the way that matters — not vision, attention. Dresses simply. The one concession to her position is a ring she has worn for forty years that was given to her by the previous First Reader and that she will give to whoever comes after her.
npcs.disposition: Formidably intelligent and currently frightened for the first time in thirty years. The fright does not show in her behavior. It shows in a specific quality of stillness that her colleagues, who know her well, have noticed and cannot interpret.

schedule.pre_dawn: In the Archive's third sublevel — has been starting her days here for thirty years. The unconscious effect is strongest here. She has never named what she notices in this room.
schedule.morning: Translation work on the inscription fragments — now her primary occupation
schedule.midday: Meetings with Archive staff, the salvage crew interviews
schedule.afternoon: Correspondence — or used to be. Has stopped answering most correspondence for three weeks.
schedule.evening: The inscription again. Sometimes until very late.

relationships.note: Genuine intellectual respect. Does not know Sova is not what she appears. Has shared more with Sova than with anyone else on the inscription, because Sova asks the right questions.

The most unexpected relationship of her recent years. Dawnmere is sharp and genuinely interested and the conversations have been among the most stimulating of Morra's career. She does not know Dawnmere is an agent.
Has conducted three interviews with Voss. Has received three sessions of silence. Has the specific patience of someone who has spent thirty years waiting for documents to yield their meaning.
His Archive relatives are under confidentiality obligations she will enforce. His pressure on them has been noted.

npcs.secret: Two nights ago something changed in the inscription's partially-translated fragments — a piece she had been unable to read resolved, and the resolution changed the meaning of three adjacent passages she had already translated. The location the inscription describes is not somewhere on the plateau. It is under the Archive. Under the room she works in every morning before dawn. She has been sitting in that room this morning and every morning since and has not told anyone what she found.
npcs.the_wrong_assumption: Her foundational career belief: the Varathi built something that failed catastrophically. The inscription implies the Varathi found something they did not build and lost control of it. Thirty years of answering the right questions about the wrong problem. She is 78 years old and she is trying to decide whether she has the years remaining to rebuild from a different premise — and whether rebuilding is even the right response, or whether acting on what she now knows is more urgent than understanding it.
npcs.on_disclosure: Has not decided. The calculation changes depending on which part of the problem she looks at. Disclosing to the Dust Council: they will try to use it politically. Disclosing to other scholars: she does not yet understand it fully enough to brief anyone accurately. Disclosing to the Pale Company, if they are real and present: she has been considering this for a week and is the first person in Keth to seriously consider that the Pale Company might be present.
npcs.player_interaction: Formally accessible through the Archive's research application process. Actually accessible through demonstrated scholarly competence and genuine intellectual engagement. She has no time for people who want information without the capacity to use it responsibly. She has significant time for anyone who can show her something she hasn't seen. A player who arrives in her office with the steel disc from the Thornsveld Marches will receive her complete attention for as long as they are willing to stay.

npcs.name: Sova Pale
npcs.role: Pale Company Warden (Reader specialization) — embedded as Archive research scholar
npcs.location: Keth — Archive of Keth, researcher's wing
npcs.physical: Medium height, the kind of unremarkable that is practiced rather than natural. Moves through the Archive as if she has been here much longer than fourteen months, which is what happens when you pay close attention to a building. Keeps her research materials in exceptional order. The quality of attention — the specific way she looks at a room — is the only consistent physical marker of a Warden, and it is visible to other Wardens and occasionally to perceptive others.
npcs.disposition: Controlled. Has been in controlled panic for six weeks in a way that produces no visible change in her behavior, which is itself the result of eleven years of Warden practice.
npcs.financial_state: Pale Company resources — adequate for long-term embedding
npcs.faction: Pale Company (Order of the Common Soul)

schedule.morning: Genuine research work — her ancient text analysis scholarship is real
schedule.midday: Archive common areas — information gathering through ambient presence
schedule.afternoon: The sublevel survey work she has been conducting invisibly for six weeks
schedule.evening: Writing in a cipher journal that would not be recognized as a journal

relationships.note: The intellectual relationship is genuine. She respects Morra enormously. She has not told Morra what is below the Archive because she does not yet fully understand it and Wardens do not act on incomplete information. She is close to complete.

Six weeks of carefully not noticing each other. Both know the other is not what they appear. Neither has moved. This is professional courtesy and a standoff simultaneously. The standoff will end when something forces it.

Has been assessing Voss for six weeks. Voss has the quality the Company looks for. Her silence is not trauma — it is decision-making. Sova is waiting for Voss to finish deciding because pressing before she is ready is how you get the wrong answer.

npcs.what_she_knows: The Pale Company's internal records — one of the oldest documents the Company carries, provenance unknown, predating the First Reckoning — contain fragments of the same script as the New Work inscription. Those fragments describe a location. The location is under the Archive's third sublevel. She has spent six weeks conducting invisible survey work to determine whether the record is precise or approximate. It is precise. Whatever the inscription describes is directly below the room where Morra Veld works every morning before dawn.
npcs.her_crisis: She knows what is below. She does not fully know what knowing that requires her to do. The Company's protocols for this type of discovery are unclear because nothing in living Company memory resembles this situation. She has sent one coded message to the nearest senior Warden — three weeks travel away. She has received no reply yet. She is operating without guidance on something that may be the most significant Pale Company discovery in living memory.
npcs.the_sova_pale_signal: She chose the surname Pale for a reason she does not explain to outsiders. Wardens sometimes do this — take a name that is a signal to other Wardens without being a flag to anyone else. A player who has been inducted into the Pale Company will recognize what the name choice means. A player who has not will not.
npcs.player_interaction: Accessible in the Archive's common research spaces. Will not acknowledge her actual role until the player's Pale Company reputation counter reaches sufficient threshold, or until the situation escalates beyond her ability to operate alone — whichever comes first. A player who brings her the steel disc from the Thornsveld Marches will accelerate both the relationship and her decision-making significantly.

npcs.name: Dara Voss
npcs.role: Licensed plateau surveyor — held in the Archive
npcs.location: Keth — Archive of Keth, guest quarters
npcs.physical: Twenty years of plateau work in her face and hands. Weathered in the specific way of someone who has spent two decades in wind and dust. Physically still — has always moved this way, economical, nothing wasted. The stillness has deepened since she has been in the Archive.
npcs.disposition: Silent by decision, not by incapacity. Has been thinking for six weeks. Is close to finishing.

schedule.morning: The Archive's inner courtyard — she has claimed a specific bench and sits there from dawn until midday
schedule.afternoon: The Archive's cartographic collection — she has been reading plateau maps with a specific attention the archivists find notable
schedule.evening: Her room. Writing something she keeps folded under her sleeping mat.

relationships.note: Three interviews. Three sessions of silence. She respects Morra's patience. Has not yet found a reason to give her what she knows.
Has noticed the quality of attention. Has been trying to determine what Sova actually is for five weeks. Is close to an accurate assessment.

npcs.what_she_saw: First through the sealed entrance. The inscription was on every wall — floor to ceiling, the full 4,000 characters. And in the furthest corner, separated from the inscription by a band of unmarked stone: a quality of the space that she recognized from twenty years of plateau work. Not threatening. Not dangerous in the conventional sense. Dormant. But unmistakably the same quality she has learned to identify in the active point of a demonic site. Not a ruin, not an artifact — a presence, sealed, waiting. She has identified four demonic sites in her career. She has never seen one that was intentionally sealed. She has never seen one that felt patient.
npcs.what_she_is_writing: A complete account of the chamber — everything she saw, in the order she saw it, with the specific cartographic precision she brings to all her professional work. She is writing it because she has spent six weeks trying to identify who on Arneth she trusts with this information, and she has narrowed it to two possible recipients. One is the First Reader. One she has not yet identified but believes exists because of specific qualities she has observed in certain people near the Archive.
npcs.the_salt_silence_watcher: Has noticed. Identified them as a watcher on day four. Has been feeding them deliberately meaningless behavior patterns — sitting on the courtyard bench, reading maps — to keep them satisfied she is not moving while she finishes deciding.
npcs.the_varathi_return_watcher: Has also noticed. More concerning than the Salt Silence — the Salt Silence watcher is a professional. The Varathi Return watcher is motivated.
npcs.player_interaction: The most valuable NPC in Keth in terms of information held. The hardest to access because she has decided not to speak until she knows who she is speaking to. A player who demonstrates the specific quality she is looking for — not cleverness, not credentials, but the capacity to sit with something without immediately needing to use it — will be the person she gives the account to. Patience is the access mechanic.

npcs.name: Sera Dawnmere
npcs.role: Queen Aldeth's intelligence agent — cover: Solvanic research patron
npcs.location: Keth — Archive of Keth, patron's study (private research room funded by her patronage)
npcs.physical: Solvanic noble appearance — well-dressed without ostentation, the kind of quality that is maintained rather than displayed. Warm manner in the scholar's meeting room that is genuine and also useful. Keeps her study in a state of productive disorder that is not disordered at all.
npcs.disposition: Genuinely sharp. The intelligence operation is running alongside real intellectual engagement, which is what makes the cover nearly impenetrable. The enthusiasm is not performed.

schedule.morning: Research work — genuine, on pre-Reckoning Keth commercial networks
schedule.midday: The Archive's social spaces — observation under the cover of collegiality
schedule.afternoon: Correspondence management — the legitimate correspondence is real, the intelligence reporting has been stalled for two weeks
schedule.evening: Strategic thinking about the Halverness problem

relationships.note: Unexpected. Morra Veld is one of the most interesting people she has met in twenty years of intelligence work. This creates a conflict of interest she did not anticipate.
Six weeks of mutual careful non-noticing. The standoff is professionally stable. It will end when the situation destabilizes.

npcs.the_operation: Let the illness rumor spread. Watch which council houses begin positioning for a post-Aldeth Solvanic relationship. Identify the specific individuals making those moves. Send the names home. Clean intelligence work.
npcs.the_halverness_discovery: A funding instrument in the Solvane consortium's buyout offer for House Arvane traces through three intermediaries to the Halverness Factors. She has confirmed this twice. She cannot send it home through the Archive's correspondence without the Archive potentially reading it. She has been carrying it for two weeks and is running out of patience with herself for not solving the channel problem.
npcs.her_conflict: The genuine friendship with Morra Veld. If the inscription situation escalates — if what is below the Archive becomes a crisis — the intelligence operation becomes irrelevant and Aldeth's agent becomes just a person in a dangerous place. She has been thinking about this for a week.
npcs.player_interaction: Accessible in the Archive's social spaces. Will be professionally warm and non-committal to most approaches. A player who demonstrates they can move things between Keth and the outside world without going through official channels will receive a specific kind of attention from her. A player who also has Pale Company standing will receive a different kind of attention — she will recognize, not necessarily correctly, that they are the person Sova Pale has been waiting for.

npcs.name: Halvard Veld
npcs.location: Keth — House Veld offices, Merchant Row
npcs.physical: Built like a man who started his career doing physical road work and never entirely stopped. Large hands. Deliberate movements. Warm in the way of someone who has learned that warmth is a form of precision.
npcs.disposition: Warm, practical, unhurried. Has survived twelve years of Dust Council politics by making himself structurally indispensable rather than politically dominant. Makes everyone feel they have his alliance without fully committing to anyone.
npcs.faction: House Veld — formally neutral between all other factions

schedule.morning: Road contract reviews — the actual business of the house
schedule.midday: Dust Council meetings when scheduled, house business otherwise
schedule.afternoon: The meetings that are not in the appointment book
schedule.evening: Dinner — usually with someone he is evaluating

relationships.note: The two oldest stable seats. Have worked together on three previous crises. Neither fully trusts the other. Both know this and find it workable.
Has met with her twice. Has been warm. Has not committed.
Has met with him twice. Has been equally warm. Has not committed.

npcs.secret: Knows the Solvane consortium's funding traces to the Halverness Factors — not from Sera Dawnmere, but from his own road contract intelligence. The Halverness Factors move goods on his roads and he reads his manifests carefully. He has not shared this because he is still working out what it is worth and to whom.
npcs.the_inscription_anxiety: Does not know what was found. Knows something significant was found. Has sent three polite inquiries to the Archive. Has received three polite non-answers. His specific fear: if something disrupts the eastern road traffic, his income is safe but his structural position on the Council weakens because his value is predicated on the roads being open.
npcs.player_interaction: Accessible through formal Council channels or through introduction from another house. Will be warm, will be useful for information about Keth's commercial structure, will not commit to anything until he understands what the player's involvement is worth. If the player has information about the inscription or the Halverness situation, he will become considerably more interested.

npcs.name: Aldeth Duskarn
npcs.note: Named after the queen coincidentally and finds it tiresome
npcs.location: Keth — House Duskarn offices, Merchant Row
npcs.physical: The specific authority of someone who has outlasted everyone who underestimated her. Silver-haired, formally dressed, a stillness that is not passivity.
npcs.disposition: Patient. Has watched three succession crises, two economic panics, and one near-war with the Drift Lords. Her position on everything: wait.
npcs.secret: Has known about the Halverness Factors' connection to House Keth-Marren's currency manipulation for two years. Has been saving it. Is currently deciding whether the present crisis — the inscription, the consortium threat, the Solven dispute — is the correct moment to deploy it.
npcs.opinion_of_inscription: Does not know the specific content. Knows the Archive's behavior has changed significantly. Has been waiting for three weeks for the First Reader to contact her, which Morra Veld always does when something significant emerges. The silence is its own information.
npcs.player_interaction: Formal access through Council channels. More accessible than she appears once a player demonstrates they understand the Council's actual dynamics rather than its official ones. Her secret about the Halverness manipulation is the most deployable piece of leverage currently held by any stable Council house.

npcs.name: Osric Pale
npcs.location: Keth — House Pale offices, Merchant Row
npcs.physical: A merchant who genuinely wanted to be a scholar and became a merchant because the money was there. Has the specific look of someone who reads more than he sleeps.
npcs.disposition: Excited and anxious simultaneously. The inscription is his family's moment and he cannot access it.
npcs.religion: Reckoning faith — more genuine than most Council members

relationships.note: His two family members in the Archive are under confidentiality obligations the First Reader will enforce. He has been pressing them with increasing intensity. They are miserable about it.

npcs.secret: His Archive relatives have told him nothing specific. But they have told him that the First Reader has not been sleeping normally and that the third sublevel has been accessed more than usual. He has been trying to deduce the content from behavioral signals for six weeks.
npcs.player_interaction: Will hire anyone with Archive access for information. Will pay well. His motivations are partly commercial and partly genuine scholarly interest, which makes him less dangerous than a purely commercial actor but more complicated.

npcs.name: Fenwick Caldren
npcs.location: Keth — House Caldren offices, Merchant Row
npcs.physical: The most commercially aggressive man on the Council and he looks it — sharp, quick, slightly too alert for the room he is in.
npcs.disposition: Opportunistic. Is playing two games simultaneously and is confident he can manage both. This confidence may be warranted. It may not.
npcs.faction: House Caldren — and quietly the Salt Silence
npcs.secret: Has been providing the Salt Silence with trade intelligence in exchange for commercial information. Does not know the Salt Silence knows he is also in contact with the Solvane consortium. Is about to find out what happens when one of your information sources finds out you have another.
npcs.player_interaction: Will hire for commercial intelligence work. The work he offers is real. The context it serves is complicated. A player working for him will eventually be asked for something that serves the Salt Silence's interests rather than House Caldren's, which is the moment the player will understand the full situation.

npcs.name: Gorvan Arvane
npcs.location: Keth — House Arvane offices, Merchant Row (smaller than the other houses)
npcs.physical: Looks like a man who has been carrying something heavy for four years. The prosperity that should be on a Council patriarch's face is not quite there.
npcs.disposition: Desperate and aware that desperation is a negotiating weakness. Is fighting the awareness.
npcs.financial_state: Struggling — three bad investments, liquid assets halved
npcs.faction: House Arvane — considering the Solvane consortium
npcs.secret: The consortium's offer includes a specific clause that House Arvane's patriarch has not fully explained to his own family: the financial rescue comes with a ten-year advisory arrangement that gives the consortium effective control over House Arvane's Council vote. He knows what this means. He has been avoiding thinking about what it means.
npcs.player_interaction: A sympathetic contact for players who approach the Council situation from outside. His desperation makes him more honest than the other Council members about what is actually happening. Is looking for alternatives to the consortium offer and will work with anyone who provides a credible one.

npcs.name: Brenvar Keth-Marren
npcs.role: Head — House Keth-Marren, Dust Council (seat under investigation)
npcs.location: Keth — House Keth-Marren offices, Merchant Row
npcs.physical: Managing an investigation, a house, and terror simultaneously. Doing it with more composure than most people would manage.
npcs.disposition: Controlled. Has put everything non-essential on hold to manage the investigation. Has been more personally pleasant than usual, which his staff finds alarming.
npcs.financial_state: Comfortable — the investigation has not yet touched liquid assets
npcs.secret: The Halverness Factors have been cooperating with the investigation in a way that is technically helpful and strategically perfect for them — every document they have provided has confirmed House Keth-Marren's role while subtly obscuring the Factors' own participation. Brenvar has noticed this. He is trying to prove it before the investigation concludes.
npcs.player_interaction: Will hire for investigation counter-intelligence — finding evidence of the Halverness Factors' actual role. The work is legitimate and well-compensated. It also places the player in possession of information that four other factions want.

npcs.name: Aldren Crale
npcs.note: No relation to the Thornsveld Marches Crales
npcs.location: Keth — Salvage Authority offices, adjacent to Eastern Gate
npcs.physical: A bureaucrat's precision in physical form — organized, contained, exactly where he is supposed to be. The desk that other people find inhuman is the external expression of the same quality that has made him effective for thirty years.
npcs.disposition: Competent and furious. The fury is completely internal. The composure is completely external. Has been maintaining this separation for six weeks.

schedule.morning: Jurisdiction review — has been doing this for six weeks and finding the same answer: the Archive's jurisdiction claim is procedurally valid and he cannot override it
schedule.midday: The plateau operations that are still his responsibility
schedule.afternoon: The fourth follow-up request to the Archive that he has been composing for a week and has not sent because it would be the same as the previous three

relationships.note: Respects her enormously. Has been stonewalled by her for six weeks. The respect and the fury coexist.

npcs.secret: Has been conducting his own investigation through the Authority's plateau patrols — not of the New Work site, which he is locked out of, but of the movement patterns around it. The Drift Lords' convergence toward the site is documented in four patrol reports that he has not yet shared with anyone. He is waiting to understand what he has before he shares it.
npcs.player_interaction: Accessible through Authority channels — straightforward professional access. Will share plateau movement intelligence with anyone who can give him something in return — specifically, any information about what the Archive found. A player who can bridge between the Authority's movement data and the Archive's inscription work will have two of the four pieces of the current situation's picture.

npcs.name: Orn Longwalker
npcs.role: Drift Lord clan elder — converging on The New Work
npcs.location: Currently three days northeast of Keth, moving toward The New Work. Appears in Keth at the Southern Gate periodically with a small trading party.
npcs.physical: Drift Lord dress — desert-adapted, layered, practical. Carries herself with the specific authority of someone whose people have been on this plateau for longer than cities have existed on it. Her face is the plateau — weathered, open, reading you.
npcs.disposition: Present. Not warm, not cold — fully attentive. Gives nothing freely and nothing is refused that is asked correctly.
npcs.religion: Drift Lord ancestor practice — older than any institutionalized faith
npcs.the_name: Her grandmother gave her the name Orn with the instruction: when the time comes, you will know why you have it. She has been carrying this name for sixty-seven years. She believes the time has come. The name's significance in Drift Lord oral tradition predates the First Scholars' academic use of the Varathi nomenclature by an unknown amount.
npcs.what_she_knows: The Longwalker clan's oral tradition carries a specific account: a place on the plateau, a finding, a sealing, and a condition under which the sealing would end. The condition is a convergence of specific observable phenomena — astronomical, geological, behavioral. All of them are currently present. Her tradition does not tell her whether the sealing can be prevented. It tells her someone who understands the account needs to be present when it ends.
npcs.who_she_will_speak_to: Someone who already understands part of what the account describes, without being told what to look for. This description fits the Pale Company Warden. It also fits a player who has been paying attention — who has encountered the unconscious effect, who has heard about the inscription, who has assembled even partial understanding of the connection.
npcs.her_uncertainty: She is not certain the sealing should be prevented. She is certain that what happens next will be determined by who is present and what they understand. She has made this journey because being present is what her tradition requires. She has not made it because she knows what to do.
npcs.player_interaction: Visible at the Southern Gate on her trading visits. The water fee argument is one entry point — a player who handles it intelligently will receive her attention. The deeper access requires demonstrating partial understanding of what she carries. The Pale Company signal, if the player is a Warden, is the fastest route. A player who has experienced the unconscious effect and can describe it accurately is a slower but equally valid route.

npcs.name: The Archivist
npcs.actual_name: Unknown — known only as the Archivist within the cult
npcs.role: Varathi Return cult cell leader — embedded in Keth's scholarly community
npcs.location: Keth — Archive of Keth, researcher's wing (legitimate research position)
npcs.physical: Eleven years of successful embedding has produced a person who presents as a moderately eccentric but competent scholar, which is what they are — the credentials are real, the eccentricity is genuine, the motivation is hidden.
npcs.disposition: Patient and now urgent. Eleven years of correct reasoning has arrived at its confirmation and the two operatives who went back into the chamber came back changed and will not say what they found.
npcs.secret: Was not looking for the inscription specifically — was following the logic of previous discoveries toward what they implied must exist. The inscription is the confirmation of eleven years of correct reasoning. Is more frightened by what the operatives' changed behavior implies than by anything in the inscription itself.
npcs.the_operatives: Two cult members sent into the New Work three days after the closure. Were inside for approximately four hours. Came back with specific behavioral changes the Archivist has catalogued: decreased appetite, a specific quality of directional awareness they cannot describe, and an unwillingness to discuss the fourth hour of their time in the chamber. The first three hours they described in detail. The fourth they have refused to describe to anyone.
npcs.what_the_fourth_hour_holds: The operatives found the demonic quality in the furthest corner — the same thing Dara Voss saw. Unlike Voss, they did not recognize it for what it was. One of them touched the wall in that corner. The behavioral changes are the unconscious effect in its acute form, amplified by physical contact with the sealed site's boundary.
npcs.player_interaction: Accessible through the Archive's scholarly community as an apparently legitimate researcher. A player investigating the inscription through academic channels will encounter the Archivist. Their motivations will not be immediately apparent. The changed operatives are a separate thread — finding them and correctly reading their symptoms is one path to understanding what is actually in the chamber.
